description: Hide or un-hide all beam elements in the range. By default, commands that use a range filter will skip hidden objects. This can be overruled with the use-hidden keyword in the range specification.

6.0.description: This commands hides or un-hides all beam elements in the range. By default, commands that use a range filter will skip hidden objects. This can be overruled with the use-hidden keyword in the range specification.

keywords.name: b
keywords.description: if a boolean type is specified, and if on, it hides all elements in the range. If off, it un-hides (or shows) all elements in the range, and the use-hidden keyword is applied to the range implicitly. on is the default if no keyword is given.

keywords.name: undo
keywords.description: this will reverse the operation of the last structure beam hide command. FLAC3D keeps a maximum undo stack of size 12.

7.0.description: This commands hides or un-hides all beam elements in the range. By default, commands that use a range filter will skip hidden objects. This can be overruled with the use-hidden keyword in the range specification.

keywords.name: adummy
keywords.description: if a boolean type is specified, and if on, it hides all elements in the range. If off, it un-hides (or shows) all elements in the range, and the use-hidden keyword is applied to the range implicitly. on is the default if no keyword is given.

keywords.name: undo
keywords.description: this will reverse the operation of the last structure beam hide command. FLAC3D keeps a maximum undo stack of size 12.

keywords.name: adummy
keywords.description: Set on to hide all elements in the range. Set off to un-hide (show) all elements in the range, and the use-hidden keyword is applied to the range implicitly. on is the default if no keyword is given.

keywords.name: undo
keywords.description: Reverse the operation of the last structure beam hide command. FLAC3D keeps a maximum undo stack of size 12.
